column6: accumulation",Developers store request data or cache entries in module-level variables without any eviction strategy or size limit,"Module-level arrays and objects persist for the entire lifetime of the process. They grow unbounded with each request until the process runs out of memory and crashes. This is especially dangerous in long-running server processes.","Use bounded caches like lru-cache with a maxSize. Store shared state in Redis or another external store. column6: loose types",Developers use the any type to bypass TypeScript errors instead of defining proper types or interfaces,"Using any disables all type checking for that value and anything derived from it. column6: wrapping",Developers wrap every single function call or statement in its own try/catch block creating excessive error handling boilerplate that obscures business logic,"Excessive try/catch blocks make code harder to read and maintain. They often mask the real source of errors by catching too broadly. Many catches just re-throw or log the same way making the handling redundant and noisy.","Use try/catch at meaningful error boundaries such as request handlers or service method entry points. Let errors propagate naturally to centralized error handlers. Use middleware-level error handling in Express or similar frameworks.","async function getUser(id: string) {

column5: per-request",Developers create a new database connection for every incoming request instead of reusing connections from a pool,"Creating and tearing down connections is expensive in terms of CPU and memory. Under load the database server runs out of available connections causing all requests to fail. TCP handshakes and TLS negotiation add significant latency to every query.","Use a connection pool with a configured minimum and maximum size. Libraries like pg and mysql2 support pooling natively. Monitor pool utilization and adjust size based on traffic patterns.","app.get('/users/:id', async (req, res) => {

15,Barrel Export Performance,Modules,barrel
column6: import performance",Developers create index.ts files that re-export everything from a module directory and then import just one thing from the barrel,"Importing from a barrel file causes Node.js to load and parse every module re-exported from that file even if only one export is needed. This dramatically slows down application startup and increases memory usage. Bundlers may not tree-shake effectively with barrel files.","Import directly from the specific file that contains the export you need. Reserve barrel files for genuinely public APIs of packages. Use tools like eslint-plugin-barrel-files to enforce direct imports.","// index.ts

column6: global state",Developers read and write shared module-level variables from multiple concurrent async operations assuming sequential execution,"Even though Node.js is single-threaded async operations interleave and can read stale values. Race conditions cause intermittent bugs that are extremely hard to reproduce. Shared counters or caches can give incorrect results under concurrent access.","Use atomic operations or transactions for shared state. Move shared state to Redis or a database with proper locking. Pass state explicitly through function parameters instead of relying on globals.","let requestCount = 0

17,Ignoring TypeScript Strict Mode,TypeScript,strict
column6: compiler options",Developers run TypeScript without enabling strict mode in tsconfig.json missing out on critical safety checks,"Without strict mode TypeScript allows null and undefined to be assigned to any type silently. Functions can have implicit any parameters. Dozens of real bugs are missed at compile time that would be caught with strict checks enabled.","Enable strict: true in tsconfig.json from the start of every project. For existing projects enable strict checks incrementally using individual flags like strictNullChecks and noImplicitAny. Fix violations rather than suppressing them.","// tsconfig.json

Performance suffers from synchronous stdout writes.","Use a structured logging library like pino or winston. Include log levels and contextual metadata. Use JSON format for machine-parseable logs. column6: complex expression",Developers chain multiple ternary operators together to handle complex conditional logic in a single expression,"Deeply nested ternaries are extremely hard to read and reason about. The precedence rules are not intuitive and subtle bugs are easy to introduce. column7: event",Developers create readable or writable streams and pipe them together without attaching error event handlers,"Unhandled stream errors crash the process with an unhandled exception. column5: stateful",Developers use the default in-memory session store in frameworks like Express which keeps session data in process memory,"In-memory sessions are lost when the process restarts or crashes. 23,Synchronous File Operations,I/O,sync
column6: file system",Developers use synchronous fs methods like readFileSync and writeFileSync inside request handlers or middleware,"Synchronous file operations block the entire event loop while waiting for disk I/O. During this time no other request can be processed. On slow disks or network file systems this can block for seconds causing cascading timeouts for all clients.","Use async fs methods from fs.promises. Read config files synchronously only at startup before the server starts accepting requests. Cache file contents in memory if they are read frequently and do not change.","import fs from 'fs'

column6: query optimization",Developers use SELECT * to fetch all columns from database tables when only a few columns are needed,"SELECT * returns unnecessary data wasting network bandwidth between the application and database. It loads large columns like TEXT and BLOB fields into memory even when unused. Schema changes can silently break application code that depends on column order or specific column names.","Select only the columns your application actually needs. Define explicit column lists in queries. Use TypeScript interfaces that match your selected columns. Consider database views for common query patterns.","interface User { id: string
